summary: Full backend + frontend testing of Wife To Be appointment booking app. Backend: 30/30 pytest tests passed covering shops, auth (login/me/change-password), 2FA lifecycle (setup->enable->mfa login->verify->disable via pyotp), superadmin admin management (create up to 4, 5th rejected, duplicate rejected, deactivate, delete, cannot delete superadmin, non-superadmin gets 403 on /admins and /settings), public slot computation (Monday closed, Tuesday open, overlap-blocked after booking, closed-day booking rejected 409), appointment types CRUD with duration validation (30/60/90/120), availability get/put, blocked dates, bookings list/patch, dashboard stats, settings mask+update. Frontend: full public booking flow end-to-end (shop -> type -> date -> slot -> details -> confirmation with WTB- reference) works; admin login, all nav pages load (Dashboard, Bookings, Availability, Appointments, Admins, Settings, My Account), stat cards render, filter/change-pw/setup-2fa/add-type controls present; logout works.
action_items: MINOR NAMING NIT (not a bug): The problem statement listed nav testids as 'nav-appointment-types' and 'nav-account', but AdminLayout generates them from labels as 'nav-appointments' and 'nav-my-account'. Functionality is fine; either update docs or align labels if strict consistency is desired.

MINOR: In Booking.jsx line 262 the slot container has data-testid='slot-grid' which begins with 'slot-' and can be mistakenly matched by tests using selector [data-testid^='slot-']. Consider renaming to 'slots-grid' to avoid ambiguity. Buttons themselves are correctly 'slot-HH:MM'.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is a single 748-line file. Consider splitting into routers (auth, admins, shops, bookings, settings) for maintainability if it grows further.

public_slots() is called both as an HTTP endpoint and directly from create_booking(). It works because it's a plain async function, but coupling route handlers this way is fragile - extract the slot computation into a helper.
create_booking is a public unauthenticated POST with no rate limiting - could allow spam. Consider a lightweight IP throttle or captcha in production.
Booking reference uses only 5 random bytes -> 8-char base32; collision possible eventually. DB has no unique index on 'reference'. Add unique index and retry on duplicate.
@app.on_event('startup'/'shutdown') is deprecated in FastAPI - migrate to lifespan context manager.
seed() is not idempotent for shops - if a shop is deleted or half-seeded it won't recreate individual items (guarded only by count==0).
No index on bookings (shop_id, date, status) - will get slow as bookings grow; also no index on blocked_dates.
seed_data_creation: None persisted - all TEST_ admins/appointment-types/blocked-dates/bookings cleaned up in test teardown. Any remaining TEST bookings were cancelled after runs.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test file at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py uses pytest-xdist (2 workers). Reference bookings can be created against the next Tuesday (Warrington open Tue-Fri 11-17, Sat 10-17, closed Sun/Mon). Note the slot-grid vs slot-HH:MM selector ambiguity when writing Playwright tests - use button[data-testid^='slot-'] to filter to slot buttons only. Nav testids: nav-dashboard, nav-bookings, nav-availability, nav-appointments, nav-admins, nav-settings, nav-my-account.
